mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals robust aggregate demand and positive corporate earnings momentum, leading to S&P 500 appreciation over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) during periods of GDP growth above 2% annualized over the trailing quarter.

mechanism_narrative: An ultra-low federal funds rate below 0.5% signals a crisis or deep recession, where monetary policy is at the zero lower bound and equity markets face severe headwinds, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:ZLB episodes historically associated with bear markets
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is non-negative or rank IC is below 0.0 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payroll employment and retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and lifts equity prices.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS rise month-over-month, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is not significantly above zero (t-test p>0.05), or the interaction term in a linear model is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation, rising interest rates compress valuation multiples and increase discount rates, leading to negative equity returns over a quarter horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over last 6 months and CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not negative on average during periods meeting the trigger condition, or the signal has a rank IC above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing output and economic capacity utilization, which typically translates into positive short-term equity returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO surprises (actual > consensus) are not significantly above zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7% indicates severe labor market slack and weak aggregate demand, depressing corporate profitability and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return when UNRATE > 7% is non-negative, or the signal has a positive rank IC in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts rise concurrently with declining interest rates, it signals improving credit conditions and housing demand, which boosts construction activity and consumer wealth, lifting equity prices over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declining for 3 consecutive months and HOUST rising month-over-month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the joint trigger are not significantly positive, or the interaction coefficient of FEDFUNDS decline and HOUST rise in a regression is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: During a consumer-led expansion, strong retail sales growth above 5% year-over-year signals robust household consumption, driving corporate earnings and generating short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following RSAFS > 5% YoY growth are not significantly positive or have non-positive hit rate over any 2-year period.
